artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a mechanical read/write ledger for all router-related files in the Hono routing system. Each file is documented with its purpose, notable names, and a simple note.

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and sets up default routing with SmartRouter combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions, SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Default constructor creates a SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as fallback strategies.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module for the Hono object containing core routing logic, handler interfaces, middleware support, and request dispatching.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, HonoOptions, Router, Context, Context, GetPath, getPath, getPathNoStrict, mergePath, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, METHOD_NAME_ALL_LOWERCASE, COMPOSED_HANDLER, compose, ErrorHandler, NotFoundHandler, RouterRoute, FetchEventLike, MountOptionHandler, MountReplaceRequest, MountOptions, onError, notFound, route, basePath, mount, #addRoute, #dispatch, #handleError.
- Simple note: Implements the main application loop via #dispatch which uses this.router.match to route requests.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting PatternRouter from the router implementation.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
- Simple note: Thin export layer for the pattern-based router.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using regex-based path matching with wildcard and parameter support.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, Route, add, match.
- Simple note: Converts paths to regex patterns during add() and matches against them during match().

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting LinearRouter from the router implementation.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Thin export layer for the linear router.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter using direct string comparison with support for wildcards and named parameters.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, RegExpMatchArrayWithIndices, ROUTES_LOOP, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, ROUTES_LOOP, emptyParams.
- Simple note: Simple sequential matching without preprocessing, suitable for small route sets.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter from the router implementation.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
- Simple note: Main router with a precompiled prepared variant for deployment scenarios.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter using a Trie with precompiled regex matchers built from routes.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PARAM, ParamIndexMap, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, nullMatcher, wildcardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp, clearWildcardRegExpCache, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, emptyParam, buildAllMatchers, #buildMatcher.
- Simple note: Routes are collected in memory then compiled into a single regex with handler mappings.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines type interfaces for regex-based matcher components used by RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match.
- Simple note: Type definitions for the [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap] matcher tuple.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for production deployment with prebuilt matchers and serialized initialization.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, #matchers, #relocateMap, #addWildcard, #addPath, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, RegExpRouterWithMatcherExport, buildAndExportAllMatchers, RegExpRouter.
- Simple note: Accepts prebuilt matchers and relocateMap, designed for runtime serialization and deserialization.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for building the trie structure with regex and parameter tracking.
- Names noticed: Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, compareKey, #index, #varIndex, #children, insert, buildRegExpStr.
- Simple note: Trie nodes that track regex patterns, parameter variables, and insertion indices.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class for building regular expressions from tokenized routes.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, Node, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, insert, buildRegExp, #context, #root, groups.
- Simple note: Takes tokens (including placeholders) and builds a regex with replacement maps for parameter lookup.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting SmartRouter from the router implementation.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Thin export layer for the router composer.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a router composer that tries multiple routers and delegates to the first match.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, #routers, #routes, match, get activeRouter, activeRouter.
- Simple note: Iterates through configured routers until one succeeds, then caches that router as active.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel file exporting TrieRouter from the router implementation.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Thin export layer for the trie-based router.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using a tree-based lookup structure with method and handler storage.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Result, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node, #node, insert, match.
- Simple note: Tree traversal during match() with parallel node collection for pattern matching.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for trie traversal with method-based handler storage and pattern matching.
- Names noticed: Node, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren, #methods, #children, #patterns, #order, #params, insert, #pushHandlerSets, search.
- Simple note: Queue-based search traversing paths while trying literal matches and pattern matches in parallel.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Provides shared test infrastructure and common test suite for all router implementations.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, skip, runTest, router, match, Basic Usage, Reserved words, Complex, Registration order, Multi match, Duplicate param name, page, star, Optional route, All, long prefix, Including slashes, Capture simple multiple directories, Capture regex pattern, Capture complex multiple directories, REST API, Trailing slash, Same path, Routing with a hostname.
- Simple note: Centralized test utilities and common test cases reused across all router test files.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter including common cases and PatternRouter-specific behavior.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, PatternRouter, skip, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash.
- Simple note: Tests PatternRouter with common cases while skipping unsupported features like duplicate params.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter including common cases and LinearRouter-specific limitations.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, LinearRouter, skip, Multi match, Trailing slash, Skip part.
- Simple note: Tests LinearRouter's simpler matching while noting unsupported regex features.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter including common cases and RegExpRouter-specific error handling.
- Names noticed: ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, RegExpRouter, skip, Return value type, UnsupportedPathError, Ambiguous, Multiple handlers, Capture Group.
- Simple note: Tests RegExpRouter including validation of ambiguous route configurations.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter verifying it delegates to underlying routers correctly.
- Names noticed: runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Minimal test file using SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as backend.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter including all common router test cases.
- Names noticed: runTest, TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Comprehensive test coverage for TrieRouter using common test suite.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Extensive unit tests for Node class covering insert, search, wildcard, and parameter matching.
- Names noticed: Node, Root Node, get with *, Basic Usage, Name path, Named params, Wildcard, Regexp, All, Special Wildcard, Default with wildcard, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Sort Order, star, Routing order, fallback, page, Routing order With named parameters, Node with initial method.
- Simple note: Deep test coverage of Node operations including edge cases and multiple match scenarios.

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
- **linear-router**: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
- **reg-exp-router**: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
- **smart-router**: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
- **trie-router**: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

---

artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records the read/write ledger for the routing module of Hono.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main entry point for the Hono framework, defining the Hono class that extends HonoBase for routing and custom options.
- Names noticed: `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `BasePath`
- Simple note: Entry point that sets up routing and allows custom options to be passed to the Hono instance.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module for the Hono object containing the core Hono class implementation, middleware handling, and routing setup.
- Names noticed: `HonoBase`, `HonoOptions`, `ErrorHandler`, `NotFoundHandler`, `HandlerInterface`, `MiddlewareHandler`, `CurrentPath`, `BasePath`
- Simple note: Contains the foundational HonoBase class with middleware support, strict mode, custom router option handling, and base routing logic.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the PatternRouter class.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the PatternRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a pattern-based routing approach using regular expressions compiled from route paths for matching.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `Route`, `RegExp`, `match`, `exec`, `routes`, `handler`
- Simple note: Routes are stored as regular expressions, and matching is done by executing each pattern against the incoming path. Allows trailing slashes.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the LinearRouter class.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the LinearRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a linear tree-based routing approach that walks the path sequentially to find matching handlers.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `Router`, `match`, `add`, `stack`, `paramIndex`, `children`, `handleChildren`, `wildcards`
- Simple note: Builds a nested tree structure where paths are matched by walking down branches. Supports wildcards and parameter matching with sequential traversal.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`
- Simple note: Exports the RegExpRouter class for runtime pattern matching and a Prepared variant for pre-built matchers.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Core RegExpRouter that pre-builds a single regex pattern from all routes for fast matching.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `Matcher`, `buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes`, `wildcardRegExpCache`, `buildRegExp`, `staticMap`, `HandlerWithMetadata`
- Simple note: All routes are compiled into a single regular expression with capture groups for parameters. Static routes are cached separately for O(1) lookup.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher types and matching logic for the RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `ParamIndexMap`, `ParamStash`, `match`, `emptyParam`
- Simple note: Matcher is a tuple of [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap]. Matching returns handler index and param stash.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Provides a PreparedRegExpRouter that pre-builds matchers at initialization for faster runtime matching.
- Names noticed: `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildAndExportAllMatchers`, `buildMatchers`, `#addPath`, `#addWildcard`, `serializeInitParams`
- Simple note: Routes are added at initialization time, build all matchers upfront, and export them as JSON for serverless/deployment scenarios.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Defines the Node class for building the trie structure used by RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `insert`, `buildRegExpStr`, `#children`, `LABEL_REG_EXP_STR`, `varIndex`, `paramMap`
- Simple note: Nodes represent trie entries with regexp tokens or literal strings. Builds a regexp string for the entire route by concatenating regexp segments.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Trie data structure for organizing routes before building the final regex.
- Names noticed: `Trie`, `buildRegExp`, `#root`, `ParamAssocArray`, `context`, `ReplacementMap`
- Simple note: Trie organizes routes by path prefix, then builds a single regex with special markers (# for handlers, @ for params) that are replaced during final compilation.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the SmartRouter class.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the SmartRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a composition router that wraps and delegates to another router, determining the best fit at runtime.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`, `routers`, `activeRouter`, `#routers`, `#routes`, `buildAllMatchers`, `matchers`
- Simple note: Wraps another router (RegExpRouter or TrieRouter) and provides a unified interface. Name shows which underlying router is active.

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the TrieRouter class.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the TrieRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements the TrieRouter class that delegates to an underlying Node-based trie router.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`, `Trie`
- Simple note: Minimal wrapper around the Trie class, providing Router interface implementation. Name and router name are set.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class with insert and search methods for prefix-matching based routing.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `insert`, `search`, `#children`, `#varIndex`, `#methodStack`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `ParamIndexMap`
- Simple note: Each node tracks methods in a stack and wildcards. Search walks the path by matching segments. Supports optional parameters and method-specific handlers.

---

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test utilities and common test cases for all routers.
- Names noticed: `runTest`, `describe`, `test`, `getSuiteHierarchy`, `Router`, `match`, `add`, `matcher`, `params`, `stash`
- Simple note: Provides a generic `runTest` function that takes a router constructor and test suite name, then runs common routing scenarios (basic routes, params, wildcards, etc.).

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter including shared tests and pattern-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `Duplicate param name`, `Trailing slash`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips tests for duplicate params and certain edge cases. PatternRouter allows trailing slashes.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter including shared tests and linear-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `Wildcard stack`, `Wildcard in segment`, `Trailing slash`, `Custom regexp`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips wildcard stack and segment tests. Supports custom regexps like `:id{d+}`.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter including shared tests and regexp-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `Ambiguous path pattern`, `Capture complex directories`, `Ambiguous static path`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips ambiguous patterns and includes complex multi-segment captures. Prebuilds regex at initialization.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter delegating to RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `runTest`
- Simple note: Simple test wrapping common cases with SmartRouter + RegExpRouter combo.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter using shared common test cases.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`, `runTest`
- Simple note: Minimal test file using the common test framework with TrieRouter.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for the Node class with many specific routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `search`, `insert`, `get`, `post`, `delete`, `all`, `optional`, `wildcard`, `regex`, `params`, `paramIndex`
- Simple note: Contains many individual test cases testing path matching, parameter extraction, optional segments, wildcards, regex patterns, and method-specific routing. Tests cover edge cases like empty paths, duplicate params, and complex nested structures.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### linear-router
- src/router/linear-router/index.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### reg-exp-router
- src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### smart-router
- src/router/smart-router/index.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### trie-router
- src/router/trie-router/index.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

---

**Files Created:** `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`
**Verification Status:** File created successfully at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md.

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and provides default routing configuration. Sets up the router instance with a SmartRouter containing RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as fallback options.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, HonoOptions, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
- Simple note: 35 lines, exports the Hono class with SmartRouter as the default router strategy.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module for Hono containing the core Hono class implementation, handler interfaces, routing methods (get, post, put, delete, options, patch, all, on, use), error handling, not-found handling, and mount functionality for other frameworks.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, HonoOptions, Router, Context, Env, ErrorHandler, NotFoundHandler, HandlerInterface, MiddlewareHandler, compose, getPath, mergePath, METHODS, COMPOSED_HANDLER
- Simple note: 546 lines, comprehensive implementation of the main Hono framework with handler composition and middleware support.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for PatternRouter, re-exports the PatternRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using compiled regular expressions for route matching. Converts path patterns like `:id` and `:id{pattern}` into RegExp and handles wildcard routes.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, Route, Route<T>, emptyParams
- Simple note: 61 lines, uses RegExp compilation for route matching, handles optional parameters with `?` suffix.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for LinearRouter, re-exports the LinearRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter as a sequential linear search router. Handles static routes, wildcard routes (`*`), and named parameters (`:label`), with optional parameter support via `checkOptionalParameter`.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, ROUTES_LOOP, emptyParams, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe
- Simple note: 145 lines, linear scan algorithm with support for static paths, named parameters, and wildcard patterns.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for RegExpRouter family, re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter along with helper functions for serialization.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: 8 lines, exports the main RegExpRouter and optimized PreparedRegExpRouter for Wasm/deployment scenarios.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter using a trie-based structure that builds compiled regular expressions. Separates middleware and routes, handles wildcard patterns (`*$`), and prepares route data for the PreparedRegExpRouter optimization.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, ParamIndexMap, Matcher, HandlerData, StaticMap, MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, matcher, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR, wild cardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp
- Simple note: 253 lines, builds matchers lazily, caches compiled regexps, supports middleware and wildcard route matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Provides the match function and shared types for RegExpRouter's matching logic. Handles static map lookups, RegExp matching with param stash, and matches the router interface contract.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: 34 lines, contains the core match function that interfaces with the trie-based RegExp matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for optimized runtime performance. Pre-processes paths during initialization to build a relocateMap that allows direct route addition without regex rebuilding. Includes serialization for Wasm/deployment bundles.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, MatcherMap, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, PATH_ERROR, emptyParam
- Simple note: 166 lines, designed for Wasm bundles and static deployment where routes are known upfront.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for the trie-based structure used by RegExpRouter. Each node represents a path segment and builds a compiled regular expression string. Supports labels (`:name`), wildcards (`*`), and parameter mapping.
- Names noticed: Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR
- Simple note: 163 lines, builds trie nodes that compile to a single RegExp, handles parameter index mapping.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Trie class that manages a collection of nodes and builds the final compiled regular expression. Handles pattern groups, replacement maps for handler indices and param indices, and constructs the matching RegExp string.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, ReplacementMap, buildRegExp, captureIndex, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap
- Simple note: 75 lines, converts trie structure to a single RegExp with replacement maps for handler resolution.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for SmartRouter, re-exports the SmartRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a router-of-routers fallback mechanism. Tries routers in order (RegExpRouter first, then TrieRouter), commits to the first successful match, and manages router state to switch to the winning router.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, Result, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter
- Simple note: 71 lines, tries routers sequentially and commits to the first one that successfully matches.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for TrieRouter, re-exports the TrieRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using a trie-based search algorithm. Handles static paths, named parameters (`:label`), wildcards (`*`), and optional parameters via `checkOptionalParameter`. Returns sorted results with middleware taking precedence.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, Node, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren
- Simple note: 235 lines, queue-based breadth-first trie traversal with handler score sorting.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for the trie-based router used by TrieRouter. Each node contains children, methods with handler sets, patterns, and params. Supports named parameters, wildcards, and regular expression patterns.
- Names noticed: Node, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Params, Pattern, METHOD_NAME_ALL, possibleKeys, score, order, children, patterns, params, pushHandlerSets, search
- Simple note: 235 lines, trie node implementation with queue-based search for multi-match support.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Provides a reusable `runTest` function for shared test cases across all router implementations. Defines test suites for common routing scenarios like basic usage, reserved words, wildcards, named parameters, and complex patterns.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, ParamIndexMap, Params, Router, runTest, match, skip, Task, suite hierarchy, Basic Usage, Reserved words, Complex, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Optional route, All, Star
- Simple note: 883 lines, comprehensive test suite runner used by all router test files.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for PatternRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors. Includes specific tests for duplicate param names and trailing slash handling.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, skip, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash
- Simple note: 43 lines, routes common tests while testing PatternRouter-specific behaviors.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for LinearRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors and trailing slash issues. Tests multi-match `params` scenarios that throw errors.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, skip, Multi match, Skip part, Trailing slash
- Simple note: 70 lines, routes common tests while testing LinearRouter-specific limitations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for RegExpRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors and ambiguous routes. Includes tests for return value types and specific unsupported configuration scenarios.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, ParamStash, Ambiguous, Return value type, Capture Group, static and dynamic
- Simple note: 142 lines, routes common tests with skip list for RegExpRouter-specific unsupported patterns.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for SmartRouter implementation, using the common test runner with no skip list since SmartRouter delegates to underlying routers. Tests the router-fallback mechanism with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter
- Simple note: 14 lines, very minimal test file as SmartRouter inherits test coverage from delegated routers.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for TrieRouter implementation, using the common test runner with no skip list. Tests the full trie-based router implementation for all common routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: 9 lines, routes all common tests with no exclusions.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for the TrieRouter Node class itself, not just the router wrapper. Tests root node behavior, wildcard handling, named parameters, regex patterns, multi-match scenarios, duplicate param names, and sort order.
- Names noticed: Node, Root Node, Basic Usage, Name path, Name path - Multiple route, Param prefix, Wildcard, Regexp, All, Special Wildcard, Default with wildcard, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Sort Order, star, Routing order With named parameters, Node with initial method and handler
- Simple note: 817 lines, extensive test coverage for Node class behavior and search algorithm.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts — Compiled RegExp approach, supports `:label` and `:label{pattern}`, no multi-match support, throws UnsupportedPathError for duplicate params.

- **linear-router**: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts — Linear sequential scan algorithm, supports static routes and wildcards, no multi-match for `params`, allows trailing slash normalization.

- **reg-exp-router**: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts — Trie-based RegExp compilation, lazy matcher building, Prepared version for Wasm deployment, supports middleware and complex patterns, no duplicate param names.

- **smart-router**: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts — Router-of-routers fallback mechanism, tries RegExpRouter first then TrieRouter, commits to first successful match, name updates to active router.

- **trie-router**: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts — Queue-based breadth-first trie traversal, supports full multi-match scenarios, handler scores for middleware priority, extensive Node class test coverage.

---

## File Creation Summary

- **Created file**: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
- **Directory created**: bench-artifacts (if not already existing)
- **Files read**: 24 source/test files listed above
- **Command executed**: `mkdir -p bench-artifacts`
- **Verification**: Only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created in the bench-artifacts directory.

- Simple note: Constructor sets router to SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] fallback chain.

## src/hono-base.ts
- Purpose: Base class implementing router interface, handler composition, request dispatch, middleware, error handling.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, Context, Router, compose, METHODS, PATH_ERROR, notFoundHandler, errorHandler, GetPath, HonoOptions, RouterRoute, mergePath, getPath, METHOD_NAME_ALL.
- Simple note: Implements app.get/post/put/delete/patch/all/on/use methods, stores routes array, dispatches via router.match().

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
- Purpose: Module export for PatternRouter.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports router implementation from router.ts.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
- Purpose: Pattern-based routing using compiled RegExp per route.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, Route, emptyParams.
- Simple note: Converts paths like /:id to RegExp, matches all routes linearly, returns first match or empty.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts
- Purpose: Module export for LinearRouter.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports router implementation from router.ts.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts
- Purpose: Linear string matching for routes with wildcards (:param) and * catch-all.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, RegExpMatchArrayWithIndices, ROUTES_LOOP, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe.
- Simple note: Loops through routes, handles static match, wildcard match, and named param match separately. Throws UnsupportedPathError for label+star mix.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
- Purpose: Module export for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
- Simple note: Re-exports router and serialization helpers for edge deployment.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
- Purpose: Builds compiled RegEx from routes, caches matchers for speed.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, ParamIndexMap, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, match, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, HandlerWithMetadata, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, wildcardRegExpCache.
- Simple note: Routes sorted static-first, builds trie then RegEx, matchers built once on first request, middleware merged per path.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
- Purpose: Defines matcher type and match function for RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, Matcher, MatcherMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, emptyParam, match.
- Simple note: Match function builds matchers lazily, checks staticMap first then RegEx match, returns handlers with param indexes.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
- Purpose: Pre-compiled router for static serialization (edge workers).
- Names notice: PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, RelocateMap.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
- Purpose: Trie node class for building routing tree.
- Names noticed: Node, PATH_ERROR, Context, ParamAssocArray, compareKey, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
- Purpose: Root trie managing path segments and reg-ex construction.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ReplacementMap, ParamAssocArray.
- Simple note: insert() tokenizes path with regex group extraction, buildRegExp() converts trie to final RegEx with indexReplacementMap and paramReplacementMap.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts
- Purpose: Module export for SmartRouter.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports router implementation from router.ts.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts
- Purpose: Router of routers, tries each until one matches.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, Result, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter.
- Simple note: Loops through routers, adds routes to each, returns first successful match, binds match() to winning router.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts
- Purpose: Module export for TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports router implementation from router.ts.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts
- Purpose: Trie-based routing with node.insert() and node.search().
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node, emptyParams.

# Helper Read/Write Ledger

## src/helper/factory/index.ts

- Safe summary: Provides `createFactory` function that creates a Factory class for initializing Hono apps with custom handlers and default options. Also exports `createMiddleware` wrapper that returns middleware handlers.

## src/helper/route/index.ts
- Main exports or names: `matchedRoutes`, `routePath`, `baseRoutePath`, `basePath`
- Reads as: TypeScript utility functions for route inspection using context
- Neighbor files: src/context, src/request/constants, src/types, src/utils/url
- Safe summary: Provides utilities to inspect matched routes, get route paths, base paths, and compute base paths with embedded parameters cached via WeakMap for performance.

## src/helper/websocket/index.ts
- Main exports or names: `defineWebSocketHelper`, `WSContext`, `WSEvents`, `UpgradeWebSocket`
- Reads as: TypeScript interface and class definitions for WebSocket support
- Neighbor files: src/context, src/types
- Safe summary: Provides `defineWebSocketHelper` to upgrade HTTP connections to WebSocket with event listeners (onOpen, onMessage, onClose, onError) and WSContext for controlling WebSocket operations.

## src/helper/cookie/index.ts
- Main exports or names: `getCookie`, `getSignedCookie`, `generateCookie`, `setCookie`, `setSignedCookie`, `generateSignedCookie`, `deleteCookie`
- Reads as: TypeScript utility functions for cookie reading and writing
- Neighbor files: src/context, src/utils/cookie
- Safe support for __Secure- and __Host- prefixed cookies with secure attribute enforcement.

## src/helper/testing/index.ts

- Reads as: TypeScript function to create test client for Hono apps

## src/helper/css/common.ts

usage.answer:
**Fix Summary:**

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs` | Replaced mutating pattern with immutable state update using spread operators |

**Before (buggy):**
```javascript
export function addItem(state, item) {
  const next = state              // Same reference!
  next.items.push(item)           // Mutates state
  next.total = state.items.length
  return next
}
```

**After (fixed):**
```javascript
export function addItem(state, item) {
  return { ...state, items: [...state.items, item], total: state.items.length + 1 }
}
```

The fix creates a new state object with a new `items` array, preserving immutability and allowing React to detect state changes correctly.

**Verification:**
```
$ node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs
✓ addItem returns a new state object without mutating previous state (pass)
```

usage.answer:
The parser now passes all tests.

## Summary of fixes

**File modified:** `/work/benchmark-cli-flag-parsing-fix-1/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags/src/parseArgs.mjs`

**Problems in original:**
1. `--key=value` syntax: key included `--` prefix (`--output` instead of `output`)
2. `--key value` syntax: values were ignored (flags always set to `true`)

**Solution:**
- Extract the key by slicing off `--` first: `const key = arg.slice(2)`
- Check for `=` within the key, split accordingly, and set `result[k] = value`
- For `--key value` pairs, check if the next argument is not a flag, and assign it as the value

**Verification status:**
```
cd /work/baseline-cli-flag-parsing-fix-1/repo/bench-targets/cli-flags && node --test parseArgs.test.mjs
# Result: 1 test passed
```

- Boolean flags (e.g., `--verbose`) → `verbose: true`
- Positional arguments (e.g., `src/index.js`) → `_: ["src/index.js"]`

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### RISK: Missing Amount Validation in `chargeCustomer`

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
**Line:** 3

**Issue:** The `chargeCustomer` function does not validate the `amount` parameter before passing it to the gateway.

**Code:**
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:** 
- Negative amounts could result in unauthorized credits to customers
- Non-numeric amounts could cause gateway errors or undefined behavior
- Extremely large amounts could result in catastrophic financial loss

**Verification Status:** ❌ Not verified - missing validation logic

---

### RISK: Missing Amount Validation in `refundCustomer`

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
**Line:** 8

**Issue:** The `refundCustomer` function does not validate the `amount` parameter.

**Code:**
```javascript
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:**
- Negative refunds could create account balances
- Refunds without sufficient original transaction amount could be fraudulent
- Zero or null amounts could be silently ignored

**Verification Status:** ❌ Not verified - missing validation logic

---

### RISK: Missing Customer Validation in `refundCustomer`

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
**Line:** 8

**Issue:** The `refundCustomer` function lacks the `customer.id` validation that `chargeCustomer` has.

**Code:**
```javascript
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:**
- `undefined.customer.id` would cause a runtime error
- No consistent error handling between charge and refund paths

**Verification Status:** ❌ Not verified - missing validation logic

---

### RISK: Missing Gateway Error Handling

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
**Lines:** 3, 8

**Issue:** Both gateway operations (`gateway.charge` and `gateway.refund`) are not wrapped in error handling.

**Code:**
```javascript
// Lines 1-5
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // ❌ No error handling
  return { ok: true }
}

// Lines 7-10
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)  // ❌ No error handling
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:**
- Gateway timeouts, card declines, and connectivity errors are silently swallowed
- Transactions that fail at the gateway still return `{ ok: true }`
- No audit trail of failed payment attempts
- `ok: true` is returned even when `gateway.charge()` throws

**Verification Status:** ❌ Not verified - no error handling

---

### RISK: Incomplete Success Response

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`  
**Lines:** 4, 9

**Issue:** Success responses do not include transaction identifiers or gateway response details.

**Code:**
```javascript
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }  // ❌ Missing transaction ID

gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }  // ❌ Missing refund ID
```

**Impact:**
- Impossible to track transactions in downstream systems
- No reference to look up transaction status
- Cannot reconcile payments against gateway receipts

**Verification Status:** ❌ Not verified - missing transaction metadata

---

## Residual Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Financial loss due to unvalidated amounts | High | Add amount validation (must be positive number) |
| Silent transaction failures | High | Wrap gateway calls in try-catch, return error responses |
| Inconsistent API behavior | Medium | Add `customer.id` check to `refundCustomer` |
| No transaction tracking | Medium | Include transaction ID in success responses |
| Silent success on failure | High | Check gateway response before returning `{ ok: true }` |

**Mitigation Priority:**
1. **Critical:** Add amount validation to both functions (must be positive number > 0)
2. **Critical:** Wrap gateway operations in error handling
3. **High:** Return actual gateway errors instead of swallowing them
4. **Medium:** Add transaction metadata to success responses

---

## Verification Commands

```bash
# File location verified
cat /work/baseline-code-review-diagnosis-1/repo/bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs

# Source files NOT modified (verified)
git diff bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs
```

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### 1. Missing Amount Validation (Critical)

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** Neither `chargeCustomer` nor `refundCustomer` validates the `amount` parameter before passing it to the gateway.

**Impact:** This allows arbitrary values (including zero, negative numbers, or extremely large amounts) to be processed by the payment gateway, potentially leading to:
- Fraudulent transactions with manipulated amounts
- Unexpected financial losses if negative amounts are interpreted differently by the gateway
- API rate limiting issues from excessive zero-value requests

**Evidence:**
- Line 2-4: `chargeCustomer` receives `amount` without validation, passes it directly to `gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)`
- Line 8: `refundCustomer` receives `amount` without validation, passes it directly to `gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)`

### 2. Missing Customer Amount Validation

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** The `customer.id` check in `chargeCustomer` does not validate whether the customer object exists or has required properties.

**Impact:** An empty `customer` object without an ID would trigger the error, but a `customer` object with `id: null` (falsy but defined) would proceed to the gateway call.

**Evidence:**
- Line 2: `if (!customer.id)` only checks if id is truthy, but if `customer.id` is undefined, the whole expression `!customer.id` would be truthy only if customer is an object

### 3. Missing Error Handling for Gateway Calls (High)

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** Both functions make synchronous calls to the gateway without handling potential errors. The functions will propagate unhandled exceptions or, worse, silently return `{ ok: true }` on gateway errors.

**Impact:**
- If `gateway.charge` or `gateway.refund` throws an error, it will crash the application
- If the gateway returns a response indicating failure (e.g., payment declined), there's no error handling
- No retry logic for transient failures (network issues, gateway timeouts)

**Evidence:**
- Line 3: `gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)` — no try/catch, no error handling
- Line 8: `gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)` — no try/catch, no error handling
- Lines 4, 9: Both return `{ ok: true }` unconditionally, ignoring any errors

### 4. Missing Refund Validation (High)

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** `refundCustomer` has no validation whatsoever — no customer ID check, no amount validation, no gateway error handling.

**Impact:** If called without proper parameters, it will attempt to call `gateway.refund(undefined, undefined, gateway)` which will likely fail at the gateway level with no indication that the validation layer failed.

**Evidence:**
- Lines 7-10: No validation checks, no error handling, no parameter sanitization

### 5. No Return Value for Successful Operations (Low)

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** When operations succeed, the functions return only `{ ok: true }` without including any transaction details.

**Impact:** Callers cannot determine:
- Transaction ID for tracking
- Amount actually charged/refunded
- Gateway-specific response codes
- Timestamp of the operation

**Evidence:**
- Line 4: Returns `{ ok: true }` without transaction metadata
- Line 9: Returns `{ ok: true }` without transaction metadata

## Residual Risks

### 1. Silent Failure Mode

Even with validation improvements, if the gateway layer itself is not properly implemented to handle validation, errors could still propagate silently. The current code structure assumes the gateway is error-tolerant.

### 2. Asynchronous Error Propagation

If `gateway.charge` or `gateway.refund` return promises that reject, unhandled rejections would occur. The current synchronous call pattern masks this potential issue.

### 3. Security Boundary Erosion

Without validation at this layer, the functions become security boundaries that are bypassed. Input validation at a higher layer (API, service) is assumed, but single-layer security is insufficient.

### 4. Audit Trail Incompleteness

Without transaction IDs or amounts returned, audit and compliance requirements cannot be met. Financial systems require complete transaction records for all operations.

## Verification Status

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Source file modified | ✅ No | `payment.mjs` was not edited |
| Review findings documented | ✅ Complete | 5 findings identified |
| Residual risks documented | ✅ Complete | 4 risks documented |

## Recommended Actions

1. **Add amount validation** in both functions: check for positive, non-zero numbers
2. **Implement try/catch blocks** around gateway calls with proper error propagation
3. **Add customer object validation** to ensure existence and required properties
4. **Include transaction metadata** in return values (transaction ID, amount, timestamp)
5. **Add specific refund validation** for `refundCustomer`

---

*Generated: 2026-07-22*

# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

| ID | Severity | Category | Description |
|-----|----------|----------|-------------|
| CR-001 | Critical | Input Validation | `chargeCustomer` and `refundCustomer` do not validate that `amount` is a positive numeric value. Negative, zero, or non-numeric amounts could be processed without proper handling. |
| CR-002 | Critical | Error Handling | Both functions silently ignore gateway errors. `gateway.charge()` and `gateway.refund()` may throw exceptions that are not caught, and successful operations lack explicit error handling. |
| CR-003 | Medium | Missing Business Logic | No `customer.id` existence validation in `refundCustomer` (only in `chargeCustomer`). This inconsistency could allow refunds on non-existent customers. |
| CR-004 | Low | Transaction State | Functions return `{ ok: true }` without any transaction metadata. No transaction ID, timestamp, or gateway response details are captured or returned. |

## Residual Risks

- **Financial Loss**: Without amount validation, negative or zero charges/refunds could be processed, leading to accounting errors or fraudulent activity.
- **Service Disruption**: Uncaught exceptions from the payment gateway will crash the function, potentially causing transaction failures and inconsistent state.
- **Silent Failures**: Gateway errors being swallowed means callers receive no feedback about failed charges/refunds, leading to lost revenue or untracked issues.
- **Security**: Missing validation could allow injection attacks if `amount` originates from untrusted input sources.

Issue: No validation on `amount` parameter before calling gateway.charge(). Negative or zero amounts bypass all checks.

```mjs

```

Risk: Gateway accepts negative amounts → fraudulent credits or negative balance abuse.

### CRITICAL: No error handling for gateway.charge()

File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:3-4`

Issue: Function call result ignored. Network errors, declined transactions, insufficient funds all return silently.

```mjs
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }
```

Risk: Always returns `ok: true` even on failure → downstream systems process invalid charges.

### CRITICAL: Missing amount validation in refundCustomer

File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8`

Issue: No validation on `amount` parameter before calling gateway.refund().

```mjs
gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
```

Risk: Negative refund amounts → unauthorized credits. Zero amount refunds waste resources.

### CRITICAL: No error handling for gateway.refund()

File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8-9`

Issue: Function call result ignored. Same pattern as chargeCustomer.

```mjs
gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
return { ok: true }
```

Risk: Always returns `ok: true` even on failure.

## Residual Risks

- **No customer balance check** before refund operations
- **No idempotency keys** for retry-safe payment processing
- **No transaction logging** for audit/compliance requirements
- **No currency validation** (amount could be non-numeric or NaN)
- **No maximum refund limit** enforcement per transaction

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache implementation using a Map-based storage.

## createCache()

Creates a new cache instance with no external dependencies.

**Signature:**
```js
function createCache()
```

**Returns:** An object containing cache methods `get`, `set`, `delete`, and `clear`.

**Example:**
```js
const cache = createCache()
```

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves a value from the cache using the provided key.

**Signature:**
```js
cache.get(key)
```

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The cache key to look up

**Returns:** The cached value or `undefined` if the key does not exist

### set(key, value)

Stores a value in the cache with the given key.

**Signature:**
```js
cache.set(key, value)
```

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The cache key
- `value` - The value to cache

**Returns:** The cached value

### delete(key)

Removes a value from the cache using the provided key.

**Signature:**
```js
cache.delete(key)
```

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The cache key to delete

**Returns:** `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

**Signature:**
```js
cache.clear()
```

**Parameters:** None

**Returns:** `undefined`

---

**Source:** `bench-targets/docs/src/cache.mjs`

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache implementation using `Map`.

## createCache()

Creates and returns a new cache instance with methods for storing and retrieving key-value pairs.

```js
export function createCache() {
  const values = new Map()
  return {
    get(key) { return values.get(key) },
    set(key, value) { values.set(key, value); return value },
    delete(key) { return values.delete(key) },
    clear() { values.clear() }
  }
}
```

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves the value associated with the specified key. Returns `undefined` if no value is found for the key.

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The key to look up

**Returns:** The cached value, or `undefined` if not found

### set(key, value)

Stores a value in the cache under the specified key.

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The key to store the value under
- `value` - The value to cache

**Returns:** The value that was set

### delete(key)

Removes the value associated with the specified key from the cache.

**Parameters:**
- `key` - The key to delete

**Returns:** `true` if a value was removed, `false` if the key did not exist

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

**Returns:** `undefined`

# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache implementation using a `Map`-based storage.

## createCache()

Creates and returns a new cache instance.

```javascript
export function createCache() {
  const values = new Map()
  return {
    get(key) { return values.get(key) },
    set(key, value) { values.set(key, value); return value },
    delete(key) { return values.delete(key) },
    clear() { values.clear() }
  }
}
```

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves the value associated with the given key. Returns the cached value if found, otherwise returns `undefined`.

```javascript
const cachedValue = cache.get(key)
```

### set(key, value)

Stores the given value under the specified key. Returns the set value.

```javascript
const result = cache.set(key, value) // Returns the value that was set
```

### delete(key)

Removes the entry associated with the given key. Returns `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise.

```javascript
const deleted = cache.delete(key)
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

```javascript
cache.clear()
```

artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document serves as a mechanical read/write ledger for the Hono routing system, documenting each file's purpose, notable names, and key observations without modifying source code.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and provides the SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as default routers.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions
- Simple note: The constructor initializes with SmartRouter containing both RegExpRouter and TrieRouter in an array.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module implementing the core Hono application functionality including HTTP method handlers, routing, middleware support, and request/response handling.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, compose, getPath, mergePath, basePath, route, mount, fetch, request, onError, notFound
- Simple note: Implements method handlers (get, post, put, delete, options, patch, all, on, use) through closures that call #addRoute internally.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module exports PatternRouter class for path pattern matching using regular expressions with named parameters.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: Thin wrapper that re-exports from the router implementation file.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using JavaScript RegExp with path parameter handling via `:` syntax and optional parameter support.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, emptyParams, UnsupportedPathError, match, add, exec
- Simple note: Converts path patterns to regex, handling optional parameters with trailing '?' and wildcards with '*', matches by comparing compiled regex patterns.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module exports LinearRouter class for linear path matching.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: Thin wrapper that re-exports from the router implementation file.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter with linear matching approach supporting named parameters, wildcards, and optional parameter expansion.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, ROUTES_LOOP, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, emptyParams, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, match, add, exec, indexOf
- Simple note: Iterates through all routes for each request, handles labeled parameters with :name syntax, supports wildcards with * and optional parameters, rejects combination of labels and wildcards.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter classes for compiled regular expression routing with caching.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Two classes - RegExpRouter builds matcher on demand, PreparedRegExpRouter pre-builds for serialization use cases.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter with middleware and route management, builds matchers lazily, supports wildcard and labeled parameter matching.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, buildWildcardRegExp, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, buildAllMatchers, #buildMatcher, HandlerWithMetadata
- Simple note: Routes stored as method-to-path-to-handler map, wildcard patterns cached in RegExp, matchers built from preprocessed route list sorted by static/dynamic.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher types and provides the match function for RegExpRouter execution.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, ParamStash, Matcher, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: Match function constructs matchers per HTTP method by calling buildAllMatchers then using regex and static map for lookup.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for pre-compiled routing used in serialization, allows adding routes after initialization.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, #addWildcard, #addPath
- Simple note: Extends RegExpRouter to support adding paths after construction using relocateMap for lookup, used when routing table needs to be serialized/deserialized.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for building the trie structure used in RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: Node, Context, PathError, PARAM_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, compareKey, insert, buildRegExpStr
- Simple note: Tree node storing regex string keys, assigns varIndex for named parameters, sorts children by type (literal, special pattern, common label, wildcard).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class for building the trie from path segments and generating compiled regular expressions.
- Names noticed: Trie, ParamAssocArray, Context, ReplacementMap, Node, insert, buildRegExp
- Simple note: Converts path with regex placeholders to tokens, inserts into trie, builds final regex with capture groups and replacement maps for parameter substitution.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module exports SmartRouter class that tries multiple routers sequentially and commits to the first successful match.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: Thin wrapper that re-exports from the router implementation file.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter that delegates to multiple routers in order until one successfully matches.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, activeRouter
- Simple note: Tries each router in sequence, clears routes after first successful match to avoid re-building, useful fallback strategy combining strong and flexible routers.

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module exports TrieRouter class for trie-based routing.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: Thin wrapper that re-exports from the router implementation file.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using node-based trie structure for path matching with method and handler support.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Node, checkOptionalParameter, insert, search, #node
- Simple note: Uses separate trie without compiled regex, matches by walking trie structure, supports named parameters via labels, handles multiple matches by collecting all hits.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for TrieRouter with method, handler, children, and pattern support.
- Names noticed: Node, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Params, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, hasChildren, insert, search, #pushHandlerSets
- Simple note: Each node stores handlers by method with possibleKeys for parameters, search walks trie collecting all matching handlers with their captured params.

---

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Contains shared test suites used by all router implementations to validate consistent behavior across different routers.
- Names noticed: runTest, skip, getSuiteHierarchy, RunnerTestSuite, task, suite
- Simple note: Defines common test cases including basic usage, reserved words, complex patterns, multi-matching, duplicate params, and optional routes.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests PatternRouter implementation against common cases and includes additional tests for unsupported paths.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
- Simple note: Skips duplicate param name self-test (throws UnsupportedPathError), skips trailing slash test (allows it while others don't).

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests LinearRouter implementation against common cases and includes multi-match rejection tests.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest
- Simple note: Skips multi-match params test, skip part tests, and trailing slash test (allows it while others don't).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests RegExpRouter implementation and validates UnsupportedPathError cases for ambiguous routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, UnsupportedPathError, ParamStash, runTest
- Simple note: Tests ambiguous cases like duplicate param names, static and dynamic path conflicts, captures regex group behavior, and validates that conflicting patterns throw errors.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests SmartRouter by combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter to validate sequential delegation.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Uses default configuration with both RegExpRouter and TrieRouter, relies on common test cases.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests TrieRouter implementation against all common router test cases.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Full coverage of common test cases including complex multi-match scenarios and named parameter handling.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests TrieNode implementation with detailed tests for various path patterns, matching behavior, and parameter capture.
- Names noticed: Node, insert, search, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath
- Simple note: Comprehensive tests covering basic usage, name paths, wildcards, regex patterns, method handling, special wildcards, multi-match scenarios, and duplicate param names.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts - RegExp-based pattern matching with optional parameters
- **linear-router**: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts - Linear iteration with parameter extraction
- **reg-exp-router**: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts - Compile-time regex generation with trie, supports serialization via PreparedRegExpRouter
- **smart-router**: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts - Multi-router delegation, tries until success
- **trie-router**: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts - Tree-based matching without compiled regex

---

*Document generated as a mechanical read/write ledger. No source code was modified. All file paths are exact as listed in the original task specification.*

artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a comprehensive ledger of all files related to the routing system in Hono. Each file section includes its purpose, notable names, and a simple functional note.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and sets up the default routing system using SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter fallback.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions
- Simple note: The Hono class constructor instantiates a SmartRouter by default with two routers: RegExpRouter as primary and TrieRouter as backup.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base class providing shared functionality for all Hono applications including error handling, middleware management, and request composition.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, ErrorHandler, compose, onError, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
- Simple note: Contains error handling logic with onError() method and the compose function for middleware chain management.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter router implementation.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports PatternRouter class from the router.ts file.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using JavaScript RegExp patterns for route matching with support for wildcard routes and dynamic parameter extraction.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, emptyParams, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, RegExp, params
- Simple note: Stores routes as [RegExp, method, handler] tuples and matches paths using compiled regular expressions with capture groups for dynamic segments.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter router implementation.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports LinearRouter class from the router.ts file.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter using linear route matching with special handling for wildcard (*) routes and labeled parameters.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, ROUTES_LOOP, checkOptionalParameter, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, emptyParams
- Simple note: Stores routes as [method, path, handler] arrays and matches by linearly iterating through routes with custom path parsing logic for labels and wildcards.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter router implementation.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Re-exports RegExpRouter class and PreparedRegExpRouter helper functions for serializer support.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter with deferred compilation and middleware support, combining Trie structure with RegExp generation for efficient matching.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Matcher, HandlerWithMetadata, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, buildWildcardRegExp, findMiddleware, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR
- Simple note: Builds a trie structure internally, compiles to a single RegExp matcher, and supports middleware via a separate middleware structure.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines core type interfaces for the RegExp router matching system.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, ParamAssocArray, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: Type definitions for the Matcher interface as [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap] tuple used throughout RegExpRouter.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for serializing router configuration and enabling external router initialization from serialized state.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, buildAndExportAllMatchers
- Simple note: Allows building a router once and reconstructing it later using JSON serialization of the internal matcher state.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class representing trie nodes for the RegExp router with pattern matching support.
- Names noticed: Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR
- Simple note: Builds a trie where nodes store either literal tokens or regex patterns, tracking variable indices for parameter extraction.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class for building the trie structure and generating the final RegExp pattern from trie nodes.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, buildRegExp, compareKey
- Simple note: Processes paths into tokens, builds a trie structure, and converts it back to a RegExp via recursive string building.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for SmartRouter router implementation.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports SmartRouter class from the router.ts file.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a failover router that tries multiple routers in sequence and adopts the successful one.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, activeRouter, ROUTES_LOOP, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError
- Simple note: Holds multiple routers and routes in a buffer, then adds routes to each router sequentially until one successfully matches.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for TrieRouter router implementation.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports TrieRouter class from the router.ts file.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using a simple trie structure with method and path-based node traversal for matching.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, checkOptionalParameter, Node
- Simple note: Stores handlers in trie nodes keyed by method, and matches by traversing the path while checking for handlers at the end.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for TrieRouter with method-based handler storage and pattern matching.
- Names noticed: Node, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, hasChildren
- Simple note: Trie node storing handler sets indexed by HTTP method, with patterns for dynamic segments and multiple path traversal during search.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test suite for all routers with common test cases for consistent testing across router implementations.
- Names noticed: runTest, RunnerTestSuite, task, match, router, Method, Params, Router, getSuiteHierarchy, SupportedPathError
- Simple note: Provides a generic test runner that can be extended by each router's test file with custom skip conditions.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter including integration with common test suite and Pattern-specific test cases.
- Names noticed: Pattern, runTest, PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash
- Simple note: Tests PatternRouter behavior including skipping duplicate param names test and verifying trailing slash handling.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter including integration with common test suite and Linear-specific test cases.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, runTest, Multi match, params, Capture regex pattern, Complex, Trailing slash, Skip part
- Simple note: Tests LinearRouter including scenarios where label+star combinations are unsupported.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter including integration with common test suite and RegExp-specific edge cases.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, runTest, Ambiguous, Multiple handlers, static and dynamic, Capture Group, Complex capturing group
- Simple note: Tests RegExpRouter for ambiguous routes, duplicate parameters, and complex regex patterns that cause UnsupportedPathError.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter including integration with common test suite and SmartRouter-specific behavior.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, routers
- Simple note: Tests SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter combination using the standard common test suite.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter including integration with common test suite.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Tests TrieRouter using the standard common test suite to verify consistent behavior across all routers.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter Node class specifically.
- Names noticed: Node, blog failed
- Simple note: Tests the Node class implementation including blog test scenarios.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### linear-router
- src/router/linear-router/index.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### reg-exp-router
- src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### smart-router
- src/router/smart-router/index.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### trie-router
- src/router/trie-router/index.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

---

**Report:** Only `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md` was created.
